home.subtitle: MEXICO
home.tagline: The home is the medicine. The craft is how we make it.
home.heroSupporting: An architectural practice in Lake Chapala, building on the oldest principles in the discipline. Durability, utility, beauty.
home.practiceHeadline: We build homes that work for the people who live in them.
home.practiceP1: Healing Homes is an architectural practice rooted in Lake Chapala, Mexico. We design and build residences grounded in the Vitruvian triad — durability, utility, beauty — because these three principles have outlasted every architectural trend for two thousand years.
home.practiceP2: The home is the most intimate built form. It shapes how you sleep, how you cook, how you think. We take that seriously. Every decision — from the orientation of a window to the depth of an overhang — answers to how the house will be lived in, not how it will be photographed.
home.practiceP3: Our work draws on the building traditions of central Mexico: cantera stone, fired brick, deep overhangs against seasonal rain, courtyards that organize light and air. We pair these with contemporary structural methods where they earn their place.
home.firmitasP1: A house should outlast the people who build it. We use materials and methods that age with dignity — cantera that weathers, concrete that cures, steel connections that hold without complaint.
home.firmitasP2: Durability is not overbuilding. It is choosing the right material for each condition and detailing it so that water, sun, and time work with the structure rather than against it.
home.utilitasP1: A home is useful when it fits the life inside it. We begin every project by understanding how a family moves through a day — where they gather, where they retreat, how they cook, how they rest.
home.utilitasP2: The plan follows the life, not the other way around. Rooms earn their size. Circulation is clear. Storage is where you reach for it. These are quiet virtues, but they are the ones you live with every morning.
home.venustasP1: Beauty in architecture is not decoration. It is proportion, light, and the honest expression of how a building is made. A well-placed window that frames the sierra. A wall that catches the late sun. A roof line that sits calmly against the sky.
home.venustasP2: We believe a home should move you the way a landscape does — not by demanding attention, but by rewarding it.
home.founderText: I came to Lake Chapala looking for a place to build a life. I stayed because of how the light moves at this altitude, how the rain arrives and leaves, how the stone here carries warmth long after the sun goes down. Now I build homes for people who feel the same pull. Each project is a conversation between what the land offers and what a family needs. That conversation is the work.
home.contactText: If you are considering a home in Lake Chapala, we would be glad to talk.
home.footerCopyright: Healing Homes Mexico, {year}. All rights reserved.
